title: PCB Design Requirements for Production
description:
Epic tracking all PCB design requirements, guidelines, and review checklists needed to take the Temper induction cooker PCB from prototype to production quality.

## Goal
Define comprehensive, fine-grained requirements for a manufacturable, safe, and reliable PCB design that:
1. Meets IEC 60335-2-6 safety standards
2. Passes EMC testing (EN 55014-1)
3. Achieves thermal targets under worst-case conditions
4. Is manufacturable with standard PCBA processes
5. Supports design-for-test (DFT) and design-for-manufacturability (DFM)

## Scope
This epic covers:
- Electrical design rules (clearances, trace widths, via specs)
- Thermal management (copper pours, thermal vias, heatsink interface)
- EMC/EMI design (filtering, shielding, grounding)
- Safety certification requirements (creepage/clearance, isolation)
- Manufacturing requirements (assembly, test, inspection)
- Design review checklists and sign-off criteria

## Sub-Epics
1. Electrical Design Rules (temper-xxx.1)
2. Thermal Management (temper-xxx.2)
3. EMC/EMI Compliance (temper-xxx.3)
4. Safety & Certification (temper-xxx.4)
5. Manufacturing & DFM (temper-xxx.5)
6. Design Review Process (temper-xxx.6)

## Success Criteria
- Complete design rules document for KiCad DRC
- Thermal simulation validates all hot spots <100°C
- Pre-compliance EMC testing passes
- Creepage/clearance audit passes IEC 60335
- First-article PCBA yield >95%
- All design review checklists signed off

## Reference Documents
- PCB_SPECIFICATION.md (existing mechanical spec)
- GROUNDING_EMI_STRATEGY.md (existing grounding design)
- THERMAL_DESIGN_GUIDE.md (existing thermal analysis)
- SAFETY_INTERLOCK_DESIGN.md (existing safety design)
- BOM.md (component selection)
close_reason: Requirements complete: Documented Safety (docs/PCB_SAFETY_DESIGN_RULES.md), EMC (docs/GROUNDING_EMI_STRATEGY.md), and DFM (docs/PCB_DFM_GUIDELINES.md) standards.

title: Electrical Design Rules
description:
Sub-epic covering all electrical design rules for the Temper PCB, including net classes, trace width calculations, via specifications, clearance rules, and current capacity requirements.

## Scope
- Net class definitions (Default, Power, HighVoltage, GateDrive)
- Trace width calculations based on IPC-2221 for current capacity
- Via specifications (standard, power, thermal) with current ratings
- Clearance rules by voltage domain and isolation requirements
- Differential pair routing (if needed)
- Power plane design rules

## Reference Documents
- PCB_SPECIFICATION.md Section 5 (Net Classes)
- IPC-2221B (Generic Standard on Printed Board Design)
- IPC-2152 (Standard for Determining Current Carrying Capacity)

## Success Criteria
- KiCad design rules file (.kicad_dru) with all net classes configured
- All traces verified for current capacity at worst-case temperature
- All clearances meet IEC 60335-2-6 requirements
- DRC passes with zero violations

title: REQ-ELEC-01: Define Net Class Hierarchy and Properties
description:
Define complete net class hierarchy for the Temper PCB with all properties specified.

## Net Classes Required

### 1. Default (Signal)
- **Trace Width:** 0.2mm (8 mil) minimum
- **Clearance:** 0.2mm (8 mil)
- **Via Size:** 0.6mm pad / 0.3mm drill
- **Current Rating:** 0.5A continuous
- **Usage:** General digital signals, SPI, I2C, GPIO

### 2. Power (Low Voltage)
- **Trace Width:** 1.0mm (40 mil) minimum
- **Clearance:** 0.5mm (20 mil)
- **Via Size:** 1.0mm pad / 0.5mm drill
- **Current Rating:** 3A continuous
- **Usage:** 5V rail, 3.3V rail, 12V fan supply

### 3. GateDrive
- **Trace Width:** 0.5mm (20 mil) minimum
- **Clearance:** 0.5mm (20 mil)
- **Via Size:** 0.8mm pad / 0.4mm drill
- **Current Rating:** 1.5A peak
- **Usage:** UCC21550 outputs to IGBT gates

### 4. HighVoltage (DC Bus)
- **Trace Width:** 3.0mm (120 mil) minimum, prefer copper pour
- **Clearance:** 2.0mm (80 mil) minimum
- **Via Size:** 1.2mm pad / 0.6mm drill (arrays for current)
- **Current Rating:** 15A RMS, 22A peak
- **Usage:** DC bus, IGBT collectors/emitters, resonant tank

### 5. HighVoltageIsolated
- **Trace Width:** 2.0mm (80 mil) minimum
- **Clearance:** 6.0mm (240 mil) to low-voltage domains
- **Via Size:** 1.0mm pad / 0.5mm drill
- **Current Rating:** 2A
- **Usage:** Bootstrap supply, isolated gate drive power

## Deliverables
- [ ] KiCad net class definitions in project file
- [ ] Net-to-class assignment table
- [ ] DRC rules configured per class
- [ ] Documentation of each class with usage guidelines

## Acceptance Criteria
- All nets in schematic assigned to appropriate class
- KiCad DRC validates all class-specific rules
- No orphan nets (all nets have explicit class assignment)

title: REQ-ELEC-02: Calculate Trace Widths for Current Capacity
description:
Calculate required trace widths for all power paths using IPC-2221/IPC-2152 standards.

## Design Parameters
- **Ambient Temperature:** 60°C (worst-case kitchen environment)
- **Max Temperature Rise:** 20°C (traces), 40°C (copper pours)
- **Outer Layers:** 2 oz copper (70 µm)
- **Inner Layers:** 1 oz copper (35 µm)

## Calculations Required

### 1. DC Bus Path (22A peak, 15A RMS)
- External trace (2 oz): Calculate minimum width for 20°C rise
- Recommend copper pour with thermal relief
- Via array sizing for layer transitions

### 2. Resonant Tank Connection (22A peak)
- High-frequency effects (38 kHz skin effect consideration)
- Recommend: Wide traces or copper pour, minimize inductance

### 3. IGBT Gate Drive (1.5A peak, <100ns edges)
- Width for current capacity
- Additional width consideration for di/dt transients

### 4. Buck Converter Switch Node (3A peak)
- Minimize loop area (EMI)
- Width for thermal dissipation

### 5. 5V Distribution (1.5A max)
- Width for <100mV drop
- Via count for layer transitions

### 6. 3.3V Distribution (400mA max)
- Width calculation
- Decoupling trace routing

## Deliverables
- [ ] Spreadsheet with all trace width calculations
- [ ] Temperature rise analysis per trace
- [ ] Recommendation table (net → minimum width)
- [ ] Via current sharing calculations

## Reference Formulas
IPC-2221B External: I = 0.048 × ΔT^0.44 × A^0.725
IPC-2221B Internal: I = 0.024 × ΔT^0.44 × A^0.725
Where A = cross-sectional area in mils²

## Acceptance Criteria
- All power traces sized for <20°C temperature rise
- Calculations documented and reviewable
- Via arrays sized for current sharing

title: REQ-ELEC-03: Via Specification and Current Rating
description:
Define via types, sizes, and current ratings for all PCB requirements.

## Via Types Required

### 1. Standard Signal Via
- **Drill:** 0.3mm (12 mil)
- **Pad:** 0.6mm (24 mil)
- **Annular Ring:** 0.15mm (6 mil)
- **Current Rating:** 0.5A per via
- **Usage:** Signal layer transitions

### 2. Power Via
- **Drill:** 0.5mm (20 mil)
- **Pad:** 1.0mm (40 mil)
- **Annular Ring:** 0.25mm (10 mil)
- **Current Rating:** 1.5A per via
- **Usage:** Power rail distribution (5V, 3.3V)

### 3. High-Current Via
- **Drill:** 0.6mm (24 mil)
- **Pad:** 1.2mm (48 mil)
- **Annular Ring:** 0.3mm (12 mil)
- **Current Rating:** 2.5A per via
- **Usage:** DC bus, IGBT power paths

### 4. Thermal Via
- **Drill:** 0.4mm (16 mil)
- **Pad:** 0.8mm (32 mil)
- **Annular Ring:** 0.2mm (8 mil)
- **Current Rating:** N/A (thermal only)
- **Thermal Resistance:** ~70°C/W per via (filled)
- **Usage:** Thermal pad connections, heatsinking

## Via Array Requirements

### DC Bus Via Array
- **Total Current:** 22A peak
- **Via Type:** High-Current (2.5A each)
- **Minimum Count:** 22/2.5 = 9 vias, use 12 minimum
- **Pattern:** 3×4 array, 2mm pitch

### IGBT Pad Via Array
- **Purpose:** Thermal + electrical
- **Via Type:** Thermal (0.4mm)
- **Count:** 20+ under each TO-247 pad
- **Pattern:** Grid fill, 1mm pitch
- **Note:** May require via-in-pad with fill

### Buck Converter Thermal Pad
- **Via Type:** Thermal
- **Count:** 6-9 vias under exposed pad
- **Pattern:** 3×3 array

## Deliverables
- [ ] Via specification table in PCB_SPECIFICATION.md
- [ ] KiCad via size presets configured
- [ ] Via array templates created
- [ ] Current derating curves documented

## Acceptance Criteria
- Via arrays sized for >50% safety margin on current
- Thermal vias properly connected to ground planes
- No unfilled via-in-pad on BGA/QFN (if used)

title: REQ-ELEC-04: High-Voltage Clearance Rules per IEC 60335
description:
Define clearance and creepage rules for high-voltage domains per IEC 60335-2-6.

## Voltage Domains

### Domain A: Mains Input (120-240VAC)
- Working voltage: 240V RMS (worst case)
- Peak voltage: 340V

### Domain B: DC Bus (170-340VDC)
- Working voltage: 340V DC (from doubler)
- Peak voltage: 400V (with transients)

### Domain C: Gate Drive Isolated (Bootstrap)
- Working voltage: 340V DC (referenced to IGBT source)
- Isolation: 5.7kV RMS per UCC21550

### Domain D: Low Voltage Control (3.3V/5V/12V)
- Working voltage: 12V max
- Referenced to CGND

## Clearance Requirements (IEC 60335-2-6)

| Boundary | Insulation Type | Min Clearance | Min Creepage | Design Value |
|----------|-----------------|---------------|--------------|--------------|
| Mains to SELV | Basic | 3.0mm | 4.0mm | 6.0mm |
| Mains to SELV | Reinforced | 6.0mm | 8.0mm | 10.0mm |
| DC Bus to Control | Basic | 3.0mm | 4.0mm | 6.0mm |
| DC Bus to Control | Reinforced | 6.0mm | 8.0mm | 10.0mm |
| Across Isolation Barrier | Reinforced | 6.0mm | 8.0mm | 10.0mm |
| Within LV Domain | Functional | 0.5mm | 1.0mm | 2.0mm |

## Pollution Degree Considerations
- **Indoor appliance:** Pollution Degree 2 (normal conditions)
- **Near cooking surface:** Consider PD3 for components near cooktop
- **CTI of FR4:** 175V (Group IIIb)

## Specific Layout Rules

### 1. Isolation Barrier Zone
- Physical slot (milled or routed) between HV and LV
- Width: 2.0mm slot minimum
- Creepage measured around slot edges

### 2. Under UCC21550
- No traces under package between pins 1-8 and 9-16
- Ground plane cutout under transformer area

### 3. IGBT TO-247 Mounting
- Creepage path: Collector tab to any low-voltage
- Pad-to-trace clearance: 6mm minimum
- Consider conformal coating for increased creepage

### 4. DC Bus Capacitor Area
- Keep-out zone around capacitor terminals
- No low-voltage traces within 10mm

## Deliverables
- [ ] Creepage/clearance analysis document
- [ ] KiCad keep-out zones defined
- [ ] Clearance rule matrix in DRC
- [ ] Isolation barrier routing guidelines

## Acceptance Criteria
- All creepage paths verified with 3D model
- DRC configured to catch clearance violations
- Design review checklist includes all HV boundaries

title: REQ-ELEC-05: Power Plane Design and Splits
description:
Define power plane design rules for the 4-layer stackup.

## Layer Stack Reference
- L1: Top - Power components, HV traces (2 oz)
- L2: GND - Ground plane, split (1 oz)
- L3: PWR - Power distribution (1 oz)
- L4: Bot - Control signals, gate drive (1 oz)

## Ground Plane (L2) Design

### Ground Domain Splits
1. **PGND (Power Ground)**
   - Area: ~60% of L2
   - Covers: DC bus, IGBTs, resonant tank connections
   - Copper: Solid pour, no slots

2. **CGND (Control Ground)**
   - Area: ~35% of L2
   - Covers: ESP32, MAX31865, ADC, safety logic
   - Copper: Solid pour under digital/analog circuits

3. **ISOGND (Isolated Ground)**
   - Area: ~5% of L2
   - Covers: UCC21550 high-side, ADUM1250 isolated side
   - No connection to PGND/CGND

### Star Ground Implementation
- Single connection point between PGND and CGND
- Location: Adjacent to DC bus negative
- Connection: 10mm wide copper bridge with 20+ via array
- Rule: No signal traces cross the split except at star point

## Power Plane (L3) Design

### Power Islands
1. **5V Island**
   - Source: LMR51430 output
   - Loads: Gate driver, fan driver, op-amps
   - Decoupling: 10µF + 100nF at each load

2. **3.3V Island**
   - Source: XC6220 LDO output
   - Loads: ESP32, MAX31865, digital logic
   - Decoupling: 10µF + 100nF at each load

3. **12V Island (if needed)**
   - Source: Intermediate tap or additional regulator
   - Loads: Fan, relay coil
   - Note: May route as thick trace instead of plane

### Power Island Rules
- Minimum 0.5mm gap between islands
- No islands under high-dV/dt nodes (SW, gate drive)
- Via stitching to ground plane every 10mm

## Copper Pour Rules

### General Pours
- Connect all: Yes (to appropriate net)
- Thermal relief: 4 spokes, 0.5mm width
- Clearance: Per net class
- Min width: 0.3mm
- Remove islands: Yes (islands < 1mm²)

### HV Pours (DC Bus)
- Clearance: 2.0mm to all other nets
- Thermal relief: Solid connection (no relief) for current capacity
- Priority: Low (routed after signal traces)

## Deliverables
- [ ] Ground plane split drawing (annotated)
- [ ] Power plane island map
- [ ] Via stitching pattern specification
- [ ] Copper pour settings for KiCad

## Acceptance Criteria
- Ground plane continuity >95% under sensitive signals
- No unintended antenna structures in splits
- Star ground point clearly defined and implemented
- Power islands sized for current density <50A/mm²

title: REQ-ELEC-06: Critical Loop Area Minimization
description:
Minimize loop areas for high di/dt and dV/dt paths to reduce EMI.

## Critical Loops Identified

### 1. DC Bus Switching Loop (HIGHEST PRIORITY)
**Path:** C_bus(+) → Q1 → SW_node → Q2 → C_bus(-)

**Requirements:**
- Loop area: <5 cm² target
- Inductance: <20nH target
- Implementation:
  - Place bus caps adjacent to IGBTs
  - Parallel copper pours on L1 (top)
  - Minimize trace length between components

**Layout Strategy:**
```
    C_BUS+ ═══════╦════════ Q1 Collector
                  ║
         [Short]  ║  [Short]
                  ║
    C_BUS- ═══════╩════════ Q2 Emitter
                 SW_NODE
```

### 2. Gate Drive Loop
**Path:** UCC21550_OUT → Rg → Gate → Source → UCC21550_GND

**Requirements:**
- Loop area: <2 cm² per gate
- Inductance: <10nH
- Implementation:
  - Gate resistor within 5mm of IGBT
  - Return path directly adjacent to gate trace
  - Differential routing (gate/source as pair)

### 3. Bootstrap Charging Loop
**Path:** V_boot → D_boot → C_boot → UCC21550_VBS

**Requirements:**
- Loop area: <1 cm²
- Bootstrap cap within 5mm of driver
- Schottky diode adjacent to cap

### 4. Buck Converter Loop
**Path:** C_in → LMR51430 → SW → L → C_out

**Requirements:**
- SW node area: <1 cm²
- Input cap within 3mm of VIN
- Output cap within 5mm of VOUT
- Inductor directly at SW pin

### 5. CT Burden Resistor Loop
**Path:** CT_secondary → R_burden → CT_secondary

**Requirements:**
- Loop area: <0.5 cm²
- Burden resistor adjacent to CT connector
- Filter cap at burden resistor

## Measurement Method
- Calculate loop area from layout as: A = L × W (enclosed rectangle)
- Or use: A = 0.5 × perimeter × average_width (for irregular loops)
- Verify with inductance estimate: L ≈ µ0 × A / h (where h = trace height)

## Deliverables
- [ ] Loop area analysis for each critical loop
- [ ] Annotated layout showing loop paths
- [ ] Component placement constraints document
- [ ] Design review checklist for loop areas

## Acceptance Criteria
- DC bus loop: <5 cm², verified by calculation
- Gate drive loops: <2 cm² each
- Buck converter SW node: <1 cm²
- No critical loops routed through vias unnecessarily

title: Thermal Management Requirements
description:
Sub-epic covering thermal management requirements for the Temper PCB, including copper pours for heat dissipation, thermal via arrays, heatsink interface design, and temperature monitoring.

## Scope
- Copper pour requirements for power dissipation
- Thermal via arrays under power components (IGBTs, buck converter)
- Heatsink mounting pad design and isolation
- Temperature monitoring point placement
- Thermal relief patterns
- Board-level thermal simulation targets

## Thermal Budget (from THERMAL_DESIGN_GUIDE.md)
| Component | Loss (W) | Cooling Method |
|-----------|----------|----------------|
| IGBTs (×2) | 35-40W | Heatsink + fan |
| Induction coil | 55W | Shared airflow |
| LMR51430 buck | 1-2W | PCB copper |
| Control electronics | <1W | Natural convection |

## Design Constraints
- Ambient: 60°C worst-case
- IGBT Tj max: 150°C, target <85°C
- PCB max temperature: 105°C (FR4 TG150)

## Reference Documents
- THERMAL_DESIGN_GUIDE.md
- PCB_SPECIFICATION.md Section 2 (Height Constraints)
- IKW40N120H3 datasheet (thermal specs)
- LMR51430 thermal design guide

## Success Criteria
- IGBT junction temperature <85°C at 60°C ambient, 2kW output
- LMR51430 junction temperature <125°C
- No PCB hot spots >100°C
- Thermal via arrays properly sized and placed

title: REQ-THERM-01: IGBT Thermal Pad and Heatsink Interface Design
description:
Design the IGBT thermal pad layout and heatsink interface for TO-247 packages.

## IGBT Thermal Requirements
- **Package:** TO-247-3 (IKW40N120H3)
- **Power Dissipation:** 20W per device, 40W total
- **Rth_jc:** 0.50 K/W (junction to case)
- **Target Tj:** <85°C at 60°C ambient

## Heatsink Interface Design

### Mounting Configuration
- **Orientation:** TO-247 tab facing board edge for heatsink attachment
- **Heatsink:** External aluminum extrusion (120×100×40mm)
- **TIM:** Thermal paste or graphite pad (0.15-0.25 K/W)
- **Isolation:** Required (collector connected to tab at 340V DC)

### Isolation Options
| Option | Rth_cs | Voltage | Pros | Cons |
|--------|--------|---------|------|------|
| Kapton + paste | 0.3 K/W | 5kV | Thin, good thermal | Messy |
| Silicone pad | 0.5 K/W | 5kV | Clean, reusable | Higher Rth |
| Mica + paste | 0.25 K/W | 5kV | Best thermal | Fragile, messy |
| AlN ceramic | 0.15 K/W | 10kV | Excellent thermal | Expensive |

**Recommendation:** Kapton tape + thermal paste for prototype, silicone pad for production.

### PCB Pad Design

#### Collector Pad (Tab Connection)
- **Size:** Match TO-247 tab (16mm × 20mm typical)
- **Copper:** 2 oz, solid fill (no thermal relief)
- **Via Array:** 20+ thermal vias (0.4mm drill) for heat spreading
- **Solder:** HASL or ENIG acceptable

#### Emitter/Gate Pads
- **Size:** Standard TO-247 pin pads (1.5mm × 3mm)
- **Via:** 2-4 vias each for current capacity

### Mounting Hardware
- **Screw:** M3 × 8mm (non-conductive nylon or with insulating washers)
- **Washer:** Insulating shoulder washer if metal screw used
- **Torque:** 0.5 N·m (avoid cracking TO-247)

## Thermal Calculation Verification

```
Thermal path: Junction → Case → TIM → Heatsink → Air

Rth_jc = 0.50 K/W (datasheet)
Rth_cs = 0.20 K/W (isolation + TIM)
Rth_sa = 0.45 K/W (heatsink with fan)

Rth_ja = 1.15 K/W per device

At P = 20W, Ta = 60°C:
Tj = 60 + 20 × 1.15 = 83°C ✓ (under 85°C target)
```

## Deliverables
- [ ] TO-247 footprint with thermal via array
- [ ] Heatsink mounting hole pattern
- [ ] Isolation material specification
- [ ] Thermal path verification calculation
- [ ] Assembly drawing for heatsink installation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Thermal vias provide <1°C/W thermal resistance to heatsink
- Mounting holes aligned with heatsink pattern
- Clearance maintained between collector pad and low-voltage traces
- Assembly instructions clear for production

title: REQ-THERM-02: Thermal Via Array Specifications
description:
Define thermal via array specifications for all power components requiring PCB heat dissipation.

## Thermal Via Design Parameters

### Via Thermal Resistance
Single via thermal resistance:
- **Formula:** Rth_via = L / (k × A × n)
- **Where:**
  - L = via length (1.6mm for through-hole)
  - k = copper thermal conductivity (385 W/m·K)
  - A = via wall cross-section area
  - n = number of vias

### Via Specifications
| Via Type | Drill | Plating | Wall Area | Rth per via |
|----------|-------|---------|-----------|-------------|
| Standard (0.3mm) | 0.3mm | 25µm | 0.023 mm² | ~180°C/W |
| Thermal (0.4mm) | 0.4mm | 25µm | 0.031 mm² | ~135°C/W |
| Thermal (0.5mm) | 0.5mm | 25µm | 0.039 mm² | ~105°C/W |
| Filled (0.4mm) | 0.4mm | Filled | 0.126 mm² | ~33°C/W |

## Component-Specific Via Arrays

### 1. IGBT Collector Pad (each device)
- **Heat Load:** 20W
- **Target Rth:** <2°C/W to bottom layer
- **Via Type:** Thermal (0.4mm)
- **Calculation:** 20 vias × 135°C/W parallel = 6.75°C/W
- **Recommendation:** 20 vias minimum, 30 preferred
- **Pattern:** 5×6 array, 2mm pitch
- **Notes:** Connect to large copper pour on bottom layer

### 2. LMR51430 Exposed Pad
- **Heat Load:** 1.5W
- **Target Rth:** <30°C/W to board
- **Via Type:** Thermal (0.3mm)
- **Calculation:** 9 vias needed for <30°C/W
- **Recommendation:** 9 vias (3×3 array)
- **Pattern:** 1.0mm pitch within exposed pad
- **Notes:** Via-in-pad acceptable, solder fill preferred

### 3. XC6220 LDO Thermal Pad
- **Heat Load:** 0.3W
- **Target Rth:** <50°C/W
- **Via Type:** Standard (0.3mm)
- **Recommendation:** 4 vias (2×2 array)
- **Pattern:** Under SOT-25 thermal pad

### 4. UCC21550 Thermal Considerations
- **Heat Load:** <0.5W
- **No exposed pad** - heat via package leads
- **Recommendation:** Wide traces to power pins, copper pour under IC
- **Ground pad:** 4 vias to ground plane

### 5. ESP32-S3 Module
- **Heat Load:** 0.5W typical
- **No direct thermal vias** - module has own thermal path
- **Recommendation:** Ground plane under module (no cutouts)

## Via-in-Pad Considerations

### When Via-in-Pad is Required
- Exposed thermal pads (LMR51430, QFN packages)
- Space-constrained high-power areas

### Via-in-Pad Options
| Option | Cost | Solder Quality | Thermal |
|--------|------|----------------|---------|
| Open via | $ | Risk of voids | Good |
| Filled + capped | $$$ | Excellent | Better |
| Tented | $$ | Good | Moderate |

**Recommendation:** Filled and capped vias for thermal pads on production boards.

## Deliverables
- [ ] Via array specification table
- [ ] KiCad via templates for each application
- [ ] Thermal resistance calculations spreadsheet
- [ ] Via-in-pad fabrication notes

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each thermal pad has calculated Rth meeting component requirements
- Via arrays fit within component pad boundaries
- Fabrication notes specify via fill requirements
- DRC validates via-to-via and via-to-pad spacing

title: REQ-THERM-03: Copper Pour Heat Spreading Requirements
description:
Define copper pour requirements for heat spreading on all PCB layers.

## Heat Spreading Copper Pours

### 1. IGBT Heat Spreading (L1 Top)
**Location:** Under and around TO-247 footprints

**Requirements:**
- **Area:** Minimum 25mm × 30mm per device
- **Copper Weight:** 2 oz (70 µm)
- **Connection:** Solid (no thermal relief) to collector pad
- **Via Array:** 20+ vias to L4 bottom copper pour
- **Clearance:** 6mm to any low-voltage net

**Heat Spreading Calculation:**
```
Copper thermal conductivity: 385 W/m·K
2 oz copper thickness: 70 µm
Area: 25mm × 30mm = 750 mm²
Rth_spread ≈ 0.3°C/W (lateral spreading in pour)
```

### 2. IGBT Heat Spreading (L4 Bottom)
**Location:** Mirror of top pour, under IGBT area

**Requirements:**
- **Area:** 30mm × 40mm per device
- **Copper Weight:** 1 oz (35 µm)
- **Net Assignment:** PGND
- **Via Connection:** Thermal via array from top

### 3. Buck Converter Thermal (L1 Top)
**Location:** Around LMR51430 and inductor

**Requirements:**
- **Area:** 15mm × 15mm minimum
- **Net Assignment:** GND (AGND)
- **Via Array:** 9+ vias to L2 ground plane
- **Note:** Do NOT pour on SW (switch) net - EMI concern

### 4. Gate Driver Thermal (L4 Bottom)
**Location:** Under UCC21550 area

**Requirements:**
- **Area:** 10mm × 15mm
- **Net Assignment:** Split between CGND and ISOGND
- **Note:** Maintains isolation boundary

### 5. ESP32 Module Area (L1/L4)
**Location:** Under ESP32-S3-WROOM module

**Requirements:**
- **Top:** Follow module datasheet keep-outs (antenna area no copper)
- **Bottom:** Solid CGND ground pour
- **Via Stitching:** Every 5mm around module perimeter

## Copper Pour Rules by Layer

### L1 (Top - 2 oz)
| Zone | Net | Pour Priority | Notes |
|------|-----|---------------|-------|
| IGBT area | DC_BUS+/- | 1 | No relief, solid fill |
| Buck area | GND | 2 | Thermal relief OK |
| Signal area | No pour | - | Keep clear for routing |

### L2 (Ground - 1 oz)
| Zone | Net | Pour Priority | Notes |
|------|-----|---------------|-------|
| Power zone | PGND | 1 | Solid, no slots |
| Control zone | CGND | 1 | Solid under ADC/SPI |
| Isolated zone | ISOGND | 2 | Keep separate |

### L3 (Power - 1 oz)
| Zone | Net | Pour Priority | Notes |
|------|-----|---------------|-------|
| 5V island | +5V | 1 | Size for current density |
| 3.3V island | +3V3 | 1 | Under ESP32 area |
| No pour zones | - | - | Under SW nodes, gate drive |

### L4 (Bottom - 1 oz)
| Zone | Net | Pour Priority | Notes |
|------|-----|---------------|-------|
| Under IGBTs | PGND | 1 | Heat spreading |
| Under MCU | CGND | 1 | Reference plane |
| Via stitching | All GND | 2 | 5mm spacing |

## Thermal Relief Settings

| Pour Type | Thermal Relief | Spoke Width | Spoke Count |
|-----------|----------------|-------------|-------------|
| Signal/Power | Yes | 0.5mm | 4 |
| High Current | No (solid) | N/A | N/A |
| Thermal Pad | No (solid) | N/A | N/A |
| Ground via | Yes | 0.3mm | 4 |

## Deliverables
- [ ] Copper pour zone map for all layers
- [ ] KiCad pour settings configured
- [ ] Thermal relief specifications
- [ ] Pour-to-pour spacing rules

## Acceptance Criteria
- All power components have adequate copper for heat spreading
- No thermal hot spots >100°C predicted in thermal analysis
- Ground plane continuity maintained >90% under signal routes
- Via stitching connects all ground pours at <5mm intervals

title: REQ-THERM-04: Temperature Monitoring Points
description:
Define temperature monitoring point locations and NTC thermistor interface.

## Temperature Monitoring Requirements

### Required Monitoring Points
1. **Heatsink Temperature** - IGBT thermal protection
2. **Ambient/Enclosure** - Environmental monitoring
3. **PCB Temperature** (optional) - Board-level monitoring

### NTC Thermistor Specifications
- **Type:** 10kΩ @ 25°C, B25/85 = 3950K
- **Package:** Epoxy-coated bead or surface-mount (0603)
- **Temperature Range:** -40°C to +125°C

## Monitoring Point Details

### 1. Heatsink NTC (NTC_HS)
**Purpose:** IGBT over-temperature protection

**Location:**
- On heatsink surface, between IGBT mounting locations
- Thermal paste/pad for good contact
- Wire leads to PCB connector

**PCB Interface:**
- 2-pin connector (JST-XH or equivalent)
- Pull-up resistor: 10kΩ to 3.3V
- Filter cap: 100nF to ground
- ADC input to ESP32

**Protection Thresholds:**
| Temperature | Action |
|-------------|--------|
| 70°C | Fan speed increase (if PWM) |
| 85°C | Reduce power to 50% |
| 95°C | Shutdown, fault LED |

### 2. Enclosure NTC (NTC_AMB)
**Purpose:** Ambient temperature monitoring, environmental compensation

**Location:**
- Inside enclosure, away from heat sources
- On PCB near ESP32 or in separate location
- SMD (0603) or through-hole bead

**PCB Interface:**
- SMD pads or connector
- Pull-up resistor: 10kΩ to 3.3V
- Filter cap: 100nF
- ADC input to ESP32

**Protection Thresholds:**
| Temperature | Action |
|-------------|--------|
| 50°C | Warning (high ambient) |
| 60°C | Derate max power |
| 70°C | Shutdown |

### 3. IGBT Temperature (via MAX31865 #2)
**Purpose:** Direct IGBT case temperature (optional, high accuracy)

**Location:**
- On IGBT tab (requires isolation)
- Using isolated MAX31865 interface (ADUM1250)

**Note:** This is a P2 enhancement, not required for basic operation.

## PCB Layout Requirements

### NTC Connector Placement
- Near board edge for external sensors
- Keep analog traces away from switching nodes
- Route over CGND ground plane

### Signal Conditioning Circuit
```
3V3 ──┬── R_pullup (10k) ──┬── NTC ──┬── GND
      │                    │         │
     ADC                C_filt     NTC_conn
                       (100nF)
```

### Layout Rules
1. Place R_pullup within 5mm of NTC connector
2. Place C_filt at ESP32 ADC input pin
3. Route NTC_signal over solid CGND plane
4. Keep >10mm from buck converter SW node
5. Guard ring around ADC trace (optional)

## ADC Configuration (ESP32-S3)
- ADC Channel: GPIO1 (NTC_HS), GPIO2 (NTC_AMB)
- Resolution: 12-bit
- Attenuation: 11dB (0-3.3V range)
- Sampling: 100ms average (10 samples)

## Temperature Calculation
```
R_ntc = R_pullup × (ADC_value / (4095 - ADC_value))
T_kelvin = 1 / (1/T0 + (1/B) × ln(R_ntc/R0))
T_celsius = T_kelvin - 273.15

Where:
  R0 = 10kΩ (at T0 = 298.15K = 25°C)
  B = 3950K
```

## Deliverables
- [ ] NTC footprint and connector selection
- [ ] Signal conditioning schematic
- [ ] ADC pin assignment in firmware HAL
- [ ] Calibration procedure document
- [ ] Test point placement for validation

## Acceptance Criteria
- NTC connectors accessible for sensor installation
- ADC noise <0.5% of range (±1°C accuracy)
- Firmware reads and validates temperature correctly
- Over-temperature protection verified by test

title: EMC/EMI Compliance Requirements
description:
Sub-epic covering EMC/EMI design requirements for EN 55014-1 (CISPR 14-1) compliance.

## Scope
- Ground plane continuity and split design
- Signal routing over ground planes
- High-frequency bypass capacitor placement
- EMI filter component placement
- Switching noise containment
- Shielding requirements

## EMI Sources
1. Half-bridge switching (38 kHz, 50V/ns dV/dt)
2. Buck converter switching (600 kHz)
3. ESP32 module (WiFi, digital clocks)

## Compliance Targets
- EN 55014-1 Class B (residential)
- Conducted emissions: 150 kHz - 30 MHz
- Radiated emissions: 30 MHz - 1 GHz

## Reference Documents
- GROUNDING_EMI_STRATEGY.md
- EN 55014-1:2021 standard
- UCC21550 layout guidelines

## Success Criteria
- Pre-compliance testing passes Class B limits
- No layout antennas >λ/10 at problem frequencies
- All critical loops identified and minimized
- Filter component placement optimized
close_reason: Requirements complete: Defined Design Rules (0el) and Loop Minimization Strategy (b7j) for EN 55014-1 compliance.

title: REQ-EMC-01: Ground Plane Continuity and Slot Avoidance
description:
Ensure ground plane continuity under signal traces and avoid creating slot antennas.

## Ground Plane Continuity Requirements

### Critical Signal Routes
All of these signals MUST be routed over continuous ground plane:
1. SPI bus (ESP32 ↔ MAX31865)
2. Gate drive signals (ESP32 → UCC21550)
3. ADC inputs (CT signal, NTC signals)
4. I2C bus (if used)
5. UART/programming interface

### Ground Plane Split Rules

#### Permitted Splits
- PGND/CGND boundary (single connection at star point)
- ISOGND domain (completely isolated)

#### Prohibited Splits
- No slots under signal traces
- No slots in return current path
- No traces crossing ground split (except at star point)

### Slot Antenna Analysis
**Problem:** Slots in ground plane act as antennas at frequencies where slot length ≈ λ/2

**Critical Frequencies:**
- 38 kHz (half-bridge): λ = 7.9 km → not an antenna concern
- 600 kHz (buck): λ = 500m → not an antenna concern  
- 38 MHz (2nd harmonic region): λ = 7.9m → slots >40cm problematic
- 150 MHz (harmonics): λ = 2m → slots >10cm problematic
- 433 MHz (ESP32 WiFi/BT leak): λ = 69cm → slots >3.5cm problematic

**Rule:** No slots longer than 30mm in ground plane

### Layout Rules

1. **No routing through ground splits**
   - All signals stay within their ground domain
   - Cross-domain signals use isolation components (ADUM1250, optocoupler)

2. **Via stitching around splits**
   - Place vias every 5mm along split boundary
   - Connects L2 and L4 ground pours

3. **Signal trace ground reference**
   - Verify each signal trace has solid ground return path
   - Use KiCad "check ground return" or manual inspection

4. **Power trace routing**
   - Power traces on L1/L4 may cross ground split IF return current doesn't need to follow
   - High-frequency bypass caps at destination

### Verification Method
```
For each critical signal trace:
1. Identify trace on L1 or L4
2. Check L2 ground plane directly below
3. Verify no slots, cuts, or split crossings
4. Document in review checklist
```

## Deliverables
- [ ] Ground plane continuity map
- [ ] Slot length audit (<30mm all slots)
- [ ] Signal-to-ground correlation table
- [ ] Via stitching pattern around splits

## Acceptance Criteria
- No slots >30mm in ground plane
- All critical signals routed over continuous ground
- Via stitching at 5mm intervals along boundaries
- Design review confirms ground return paths
close_reason: Created TDD tests: validators/ground_plane.py and test_ground_plane.py with slot length, signal ground reference, star ground, and via stitching validation

title: REQ-EMC-02: Bypass Capacitor Placement Strategy
description:
Define high-frequency bypass capacitor placement for all ICs and power domains.

## Bypass Capacitor Requirements

### General Rules
1. Place caps as close as possible to IC power pins (<3mm ideal)
2. Minimize loop area: VCC → Cap → GND → IC_GND
3. Use multiple values for wideband filtering
4. Via directly at cap ground pad (not routed away)

### Component-Specific Placement

#### 1. ESP32-S3-WROOM Module
**Power Pins:** 3V3 (multiple pins)
**Capacitors:**
- 10µF (bulk): Within 5mm of module
- 100nF (HF): At each power pin group (2-3 caps)
- 10nF (VHF): Optional, for WiFi noise

**Placement Priority:**
1. 100nF at pin 2 (3V3)
2. 100nF at pin 37 (VDD33)
3. 10µF bulk near module edge

#### 2. UCC21550 Gate Driver
**Power Pins:** VCCI (pin 1), VDDA (pin 9), VDDB (pin 16)

**Low-side (VCCI):**
- 1µF: Within 2mm of VCCI pin
- 100nF: Parallel with 1µF

**High-side (VDDA - Bootstrap):**
- C_BOOT (10µF): Within 3mm of VBS-VS pins
- 100nF: Parallel for HF bypass

**Isolated side (VDDB):**
- 1µF: Within 2mm of VDDB pin
- 100nF: Parallel

#### 3. MAX31865 RTD Interface (×2)
**Power Pin:** VDD (3.3V)
**Capacitors:**
- 100nF: Within 2mm of VDD pin
- 10µF: Within 5mm (can be shared if close)

**VREF Bypass:**
- 100nF at VREFOUT pin
- Critical for ADC accuracy

#### 4. LMR51430 Buck Converter
**Input (VIN - High Voltage):**
- 2.2µF × 2 (ceramic): Within 3mm of VIN
- Critical for input ripple and EMI

**Output (5V):**
- 22µF × 2 (ceramic): Within 3mm of VOUT
- ESR <10mΩ required

**Bootstrap (CBOOT):**
- 100nF: At BOOT pin, within 2mm

#### 5. XC6220 LDO
**Input (5V):**
- 1µF: Within 3mm of VIN

**Output (3.3V):**
- 1µF: Within 3mm of VOUT
- Low ESR ceramic required for stability

#### 6. ADUM1250 I2C Isolator
**Side 1 (VDD1):**
- 100nF: Within 2mm

**Side 2 (VDD2):**
- 100nF: Within 2mm

#### 7. Safety Logic ICs (74HC series)
**Each IC:**
- 100nF: Shared per 2-3 ICs if clustered
- Within 5mm of VCC pin

## Capacitor Via Strategy

### Optimal Via Placement
```
    VCC ────┬──── IC_VCC
            │
         [CAP]
            │
    GND ◄───┴──── (via directly at cap pad)
```

### Ground Via Rules
- Via at cap GND pad, not routed away
- Multiple vias for bulk caps (10µF+)
- Via to L2 ground plane (not L3 power)

## Decoupling Capacitor Values

| Value | Frequency Range | Usage |
|-------|-----------------|-------|
| 10µF | DC-100kHz | Bulk decoupling |
| 1µF | 100kHz-1MHz | Power stage |
| 100nF | 1MHz-100MHz | General HF |
| 10nF | 10MHz-1GHz | VHF (WiFi/RF) |
| 1nF | >100MHz | Optional EMI |

## Deliverables
- [ ] Capacitor placement map (annotated layout)
- [ ] BOM with cap values and packages
- [ ] Placement priority table by IC
- [ ] Ground via strategy document

## Acceptance Criteria
- All IC power pins have 100nF within 3mm
- Bulk caps (10µF+) within 5mm of ICs
- Ground vias directly at cap pads
- No caps sharing long ground routes
close_reason: Created TDD tests: validators/bypass_caps.py and test_bypass_caps.py with decoupling distance, loop area, via placement, and component-specific requirements (ESP32, UCC21550, MAX31865, LMR51430)

title: REQ-EMC-03: EMI Filter Layout Requirements
description:
Define EMI filter component placement and layout for AC input filtering.

## EMI Filter Components

### Filter Topology (from GROUNDING_EMI_STRATEGY.md)
```
AC LINE ────┬──[FUSE]──┬──[L_DM]──┬──[L_CM]──┬────── To Rectifier
            │          │          │          │
NEUTRAL ──┬─┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼────── 
          │ │          │          │          │
         MOV         C_X1       C_Y1       C_X2
          │ │          │          │          │
PE ───────┴─┴──────────┴──────────┴──────────┴──────
```

### Component Values
| Component | Value | Rating | Package |
|-----------|-------|--------|---------|
| FUSE | 15A | 250VAC | 5×20mm cartridge |
| MOV | 275V | 10kA | Radial 14mm |
| L_DM | 470µH | 15A | Toroidal (hand-wound or off-shelf) |
| L_CM | 10mH | 15A | Common-mode choke |
| C_X1, C_X2 | 470nF | 275VAC | X2 safety, radial |
| C_Y1, C_Y2 | 2.2nF | 300VAC | Y2 safety, radial |

## Layout Requirements

### 1. Filter Placement
- **Location:** Near AC input connector
- **Orientation:** Signal flow left-to-right (input → output)
- **Spacing:** Maintain >6mm between L/N and PE traces

### 2. Common-Mode Choke (L_CM)
- Place after X-caps in signal flow
- Minimize trace length between choke and X-caps
- Mount flat on PCB or standoff mount

### 3. X-Capacitors (C_X1, C_X2)
- Place immediately after DM inductor / before CM choke
- Short, fat traces to L and N
- No ground connection (line-to-neutral only)

### 4. Y-Capacitors (C_Y1, C_Y2)
- Connect line and neutral to PE
- Place after CM choke (closest to filter output)
- Short, wide traces to PE
- Maximum 4.4nF total for <3.5mA leakage

### 5. MOV Placement
- At AC input, before fuse or in parallel with fuse
- Short leads to L, N (minimize inductance)
- Allow for thermal expansion (may get hot during surge)

### 6. Ground Connections
- Y-caps to chassis PE (star ground at input)
- CM choke center tap to PE (if available)
- Keep PE path short and direct

## Trace Width Requirements

| Net | Current | Min Width (2oz) | Recommended |
|-----|---------|-----------------|-------------|
| AC_L | 15A RMS | 5mm | Pour |
| AC_N | 15A RMS | 5mm | Pour |
| PE | Fault current | 3mm | 5mm |

## Clearance Requirements

| Boundary | Clearance | Creepage |
|----------|-----------|----------|
| L to N | 2mm | 3mm |
| L/N to PE | 3mm | 4mm |
| L/N to DC bus | 4mm | 6mm |

## Layout Optimization for Attenuation

### DM Filter Performance
- Minimize loop area in L-C_X-N path
- Use ground plane under filter (improves HF)

### CM Filter Performance
- Symmetric layout (L and N paths equal length)
- Y-caps directly to PE plane/trace
- CM choke properly mounted (no air gap issues)

## Deliverables
- [ ] EMI filter schematic snippet
- [ ] Component placement drawing
- [ ] Trace width and clearance table
- [ ] PE connection strategy

## Acceptance Criteria
- Filter components in logical flow order
- Y-cap total <4.4nF
- All clearances meet IEC 60335
- PE path resistance <100mΩ
close_reason: Created TDD tests: validators/emi_filter.py and test_emi_filter.py with signal flow, component order, X-cap/Y-cap placement, MOV, CM choke, and PE trace validation

title: REQ-EMC-04: Switching Node Containment
description:
Contain high dV/dt switching nodes to minimize radiated EMI.

## High dV/dt Nodes Identified

### 1. Half-Bridge Switch Node (SW)
**Location:** Junction of Q1 emitter and Q2 collector
**Characteristics:**
- Voltage swing: 0V to 340V DC
- dV/dt: ~50 V/ns during switching
- Frequency: 38 kHz fundamental
- Ringing: 1-10 MHz during transitions

### 2. Buck Converter SW (LMR51430)
**Location:** SW pin of buck regulator
**Characteristics:**
- Voltage swing: 0V to 170V (or Vin)
- dV/dt: ~10 V/ns
- Frequency: ~600 kHz
- Harmonics: >100 MHz

### 3. Gate Drive Outputs
**Location:** UCC21550 OUTA, OUTB
**Characteristics:**
- Voltage swing: 0V to 20V
- dV/dt: ~5 V/ns
- Frequency: 38 kHz
- Rise/fall: <50ns

## Containment Strategies

### 1. Minimize Node Area (Antenna Effect)
**Rule:** SW node copper area < 1 cm²

**Implementation:**
- Keep SW trace as short as possible
- No copper pour on SW net
- Direct connection: IGBT → snubber → coil connector
- Avoid unnecessary vias

### 2. Ground Plane Shielding
**Strategy:** Ensure ground plane exists directly under/adjacent to SW nodes

**Half-Bridge SW:**
- Ground pour on L4 under SW area
- Via stitching around SW perimeter (5mm spacing)
- Ground guard ring if space permits

**Buck SW:**
- Ground pour on L2 under LMR51430
- No ground cutout under SW pin
- Adjacent ground via at SW pin

### 3. Snubber Placement
**Purpose:** Reduce ringing and dV/dt

**Half-Bridge RC Snubber:**
- Location: Directly across each IGBT (C-E)
- Values: 1-10nF + 10-47Ω
- Placement: Within 10mm of IGBT

**Buck Snubber:**
- Built into LMR51430 (internal)
- External snubber optional

### 4. No Power Plane Under SW Nodes
**Rule:** Do not route 5V or 3.3V power island under:
- Half-bridge SW node
- Buck converter SW pin
- Gate drive traces

**Reason:** Capacitive coupling injects noise into power rails

## Layout Specifics

### Half-Bridge SW Node
```
        Q1 (High-side IGBT)
            │
    ┌───────┴───────┐
    │   SW NODE     │  ← Keep this area SMALL
    │   (< 1 cm²)   │  ← No pour, short trace
    └───────┬───────┘
            │
    ┌───────┴───────┐
    │   SNUBBER     │  ← Within 10mm
    │   (RC)        │
    └───────┬───────┘
            │
            ├──────── To Resonant Tank Connector
            │
        Q2 (Low-side IGBT)
```

### Buck Converter SW Node
```
         VIN
          │
     [C_IN] ← Within 3mm
          │
     ┌────┴────┐
     │LMR51430 │
     │   SW ───┼──┬── [L_BUCK]
     └─────────┘  │      │
                  │   [C_OUT]
             Area < 0.5 cm²
```

## Deliverables
- [ ] SW node area measurements
- [ ] Snubber placement verification
- [ ] Ground shielding layout
- [ ] Power plane cutout verification

## Acceptance Criteria
- Half-bridge SW node area <1 cm²
- Buck SW node area <0.5 cm²
- Ground plane under all SW nodes
- No power plane under SW nodes
- Snubbers within 10mm of switches
close_reason: Created TDD tests: validators/switching_nodes.py and test_switching_nodes.py with SW node area, ground shielding, via stitching, power plane clearance, snubber placement, and trace routing validation

title: Safety & Certification Requirements
description:
Sub-epic covering safety design requirements for IEC 60335-2-6 certification.

## Scope
- Creepage/clearance requirements per voltage domain
- Isolation barrier design and verification
- Safety marking requirements on PCB
- Protection circuit layout (OCP, OVP, thermal)
- Earth ground connections

## Applicable Standards
- IEC 60335-1: General safety requirements
- IEC 60335-2-6: Cooking appliances
- IEC 61010-1: Measurement equipment (for control circuits)

## Key Safety Requirements
1. Touch current <3.5mA (Y-cap leakage)
2. Reinforced insulation across HV-LV barrier
3. Proper earth bonding
4. Over-temperature protection
5. Over-current protection

## Reference Documents
- GROUNDING_EMI_STRATEGY.md (Section 8 - IEC compliance)
- SAFETY_INTERLOCK_DESIGN.md
- UCC21550 isolation specifications

## Success Criteria
- All creepage/clearance requirements met and documented
- Isolation barriers pass hi-pot test
- Safety markings comply with standards
- Protection circuits verified by simulation and test
close_reason: Requirements complete: Defined Design Rules (f8v) and Marking Requirements (4cm) for IEC 60335 compliance.

title: REQ-SAFE-01: Creepage/Clearance Verification Matrix
description:
Create comprehensive creepage/clearance verification matrix for all HV boundaries.

## Voltage Domains

### Domain Definitions
| Domain | Voltage | Reference | Safety Class |
|--------|---------|-----------|--------------|
| MAINS | 240VAC (340V peak) | Earth (PE) | Hazardous |
| DC_BUS | 340VDC (from doubler) | PGND | Hazardous |
| BOOTSTRAP | 340VDC (floating) | IGBT source | Hazardous |
| LV_CONTROL | 3.3V/5V/12V | CGND | SELV |
| ISOLATED | Floating | ISOGND | SELV |

## IEC 60335 Requirements

### Basic Insulation (Single Fault Safe)
| Working Voltage | Min Clearance | Min Creepage |
|-----------------|---------------|--------------|
| 150V | 1.5mm | 2.0mm |
| 300V | 3.0mm | 4.0mm |
| 400V | 4.0mm | 5.0mm |
| 500V | 5.0mm | 6.3mm |

### Reinforced Insulation (No Single Fault)
| Working Voltage | Min Clearance | Min Creepage |
|-----------------|---------------|--------------|
| 150V | 3.0mm | 4.0mm |
| 300V | 6.0mm | 8.0mm |
| 400V | 8.0mm | 10.0mm |
| 500V | 10.0mm | 12.5mm |

## Verification Matrix

### Boundary: MAINS to LV_CONTROL
| Location | Insulation | Required | Design | Status |
|----------|------------|----------|--------|--------|
| AC connector to CGND trace | Reinforced | 6mm/8mm | 10mm | [ ] |
| AC connector to ESP32 | Reinforced | 6mm/8mm | 15mm+ | [ ] |
| Fuse holder to logic | Reinforced | 6mm/8mm | 10mm | [ ] |

### Boundary: DC_BUS to LV_CONTROL
| Location | Insulation | Required | Design | Status |
|----------|------------|----------|--------|--------|
| Bus cap terminal to CGND | Reinforced | 6mm/8mm | 10mm | [ ] |
| IGBT collector to gate driver low-side | Basic | 4mm/5mm | 6mm | [ ] |
| Rectifier diode to control trace | Reinforced | 6mm/8mm | 10mm | [ ] |

### Boundary: DC_BUS to BOOTSTRAP (Across UCC21550)
| Location | Insulation | Required | Design | Status |
|----------|------------|----------|--------|--------|
| Pin 1-8 to Pin 9-16 | Reinforced | 8mm/10mm | Internal | [ ] |
| PCB trace under UCC21550 | Reinforced | 8mm/10mm | No trace | [ ] |
| VCCI plane to VBS plane | Reinforced | 8mm/10mm | Separate | [ ] |

### Boundary: IGBT Tab to Heatsink/PE
| Location | Insulation | Required | Design | Status |
|----------|------------|----------|--------|--------|
| TO-247 tab to heatsink | Basic | 4mm/5mm | Pad gap | [ ] |
| Tab to any LV trace | Reinforced | 8mm/10mm | 10mm+ | [ ] |
| Mounting screw to trace | Basic | 4mm/5mm | >6mm | [ ] |

### Boundary: ISOLATED to CONTROL (Across ADUM1250)
| Location | Insulation | Required | Design | Status |
|----------|------------|----------|--------|--------|
| VDD1 pins to VDD2 pins | Reinforced | 6mm/8mm | Internal | [ ] |
| GND1 traces to GND2 traces | Reinforced | 6mm/8mm | 10mm | [ ] |
| SDA1 to SDA2 | Reinforced | 6mm/8mm | Internal | [ ] |

## Measurement Methodology

### Clearance (Through Air)
- Measure shortest straight-line distance through air
- Account for component body intrusion
- Use KiCad 3D viewer for verification

### Creepage (Over Surface)
- Measure shortest path along PCB surface
- Path goes around obstacles (slots, ribs)
- Slots <1mm wide don't count as interruption
- Use physical path measurement on layout

### Pollution Degree Adjustment
- PD2 (indoor, normal): Standard values
- PD3 (cooking area): Increase creepage by 25%

## Slot Design for Creepage Extension
```
    HV Trace                    LV Trace
        │                           │
        │    ┌─────────────────┐    │
        │    │   SLOT (2mm)    │    │
        │    │   for creepage  │    │
        │    │   extension     │    │
        │    └─────────────────┘    │
        │                           │
    Creepage path goes AROUND slot
```

## Deliverables
- [ ] Complete verification matrix (all boundaries)
- [ ] Annotated layout with measurements
- [ ] Slot locations and dimensions
- [ ] Certification readiness checklist

## Acceptance Criteria
- All boundaries meet or exceed IEC 60335 requirements
- Measurements documented with method
- No paths rely on conformal coating alone
- Design margin >20% on all boundaries
close_reason: Created TDD tests for Safety requirements: clearance.py, isolation.py, markings.py validators and corresponding test files with IEC 60335 compliance checks

title: REQ-SAFE-02: Isolation Barrier PCB Implementation
description:
Implement isolation barriers on PCB for safety compliance.

## Isolation Barriers Required

### 1. Main HV-LV Barrier
**Purpose:** Separate hazardous DC bus from SELV control circuits
**Location:** Between Zone A/B (Power) and Zone C/D (Control)

**Implementation:**
- Minimum 10mm clearance zone
- 2mm routed slot along barrier
- No traces crossing barrier (except through isolation components)
- Ground plane split at barrier

### 2. Gate Driver Isolation (UCC21550)
**Purpose:** Provide galvanic isolation for high-side gate drive
**Location:** Within UCC21550 package

**Implementation:**
- No traces under transformer area (pins 5-12 region)
- Ground plane cutout under package center
- Separate power domains: VCCI (low-side), VDDA/VDDB (isolated)

### 3. I2C Isolator (ADUM1250)
**Purpose:** Isolate I2C bus for IGBT temperature sensing
**Location:** Between control (CGND) and isolated (ISOGND) domains

**Implementation:**
- 10mm clearance between isolated sides
- Ground plane split under ADUM1250
- Separate power supplies each side

## PCB Slot Design

### Main Isolation Slot
```
    POWER ZONE (PGND)              CONTROL ZONE (CGND)
           │                              │
    ═══════╪══════════════════════════════╪════════
           │                              │
           │      ┌────────────────┐      │
           │      │                │      │
           │      │   2mm SLOT     │      │
           │      │   (routed)     │      │
           │      │                │      │
           │      └────────────────┘      │
           │                              │
    Creepage follows slot edge
```

### Slot Specifications
| Slot | Width | Length | Purpose |
|------|-------|--------|---------|
| Main barrier | 2.0mm | Full board width | HV-LV isolation |
| Under UCC21550 | 1.5mm | 10mm | Transformer area |
| Under ADUM1250 | 1.5mm | 8mm | Isolator area |

## Ground Plane Implementation

### L2 Ground Plane at Barriers
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                         │
│   ┌────────────────────┐      ┌────────────────────┐   │
│   │       PGND         │      │       CGND         │   │
│   │  (Power Ground)    │      │  (Control Ground)  │   │
│   │                    │      │                    │   │
│   │                    │      │                    │   │
│   └─────────┬──────────┘      └──────────┬─────────┘   │
│             │                            │             │
│             └────────────┬───────────────┘             │
│                          │                             │
│                   [STAR POINT]                         │
│             (Single narrow connection)                 │
│                                                        │
│   ┌────────────────────┐                              │
│   │      ISOGND        │  (NO connection to PGND/CGND)│
│   │  (Isolated GND)    │                              │
│   └────────────────────┘                              │
│                                                        │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Component Keep-Out Zones

### Around Isolation Components
| Component | Keep-out radius | Reason |
|-----------|-----------------|--------|
| UCC21550 | 5mm | Transformer isolation |
| ADUM1250 | 5mm | Capacitive isolation |
| CT connector | 3mm | Magnetic isolation |

### Around High-Voltage
| Component | Keep-out to LV | Reason |
|-----------|---------------|--------|
| IGBT collector pad | 10mm | HV tab |
| Bus capacitor terminals | 10mm | 340VDC |
| Rectifier diodes | 8mm | HV switching |

## Hi-Pot Test Points

### Test Access
Provide test points for hi-pot testing during certification:
| Test | Point A | Point B | Voltage |
|------|---------|---------|---------|
| Mains-SELV | AC_L pad | CGND pad | 3000VAC |
| DC-SELV | DC_BUS+ pad | CGND pad | 2500VDC |
| Isolation | VCCI pad | VDDB pad | 5700VAC |

## Deliverables
- [ ] Isolation barrier zone definition
- [ ] Slot routing specifications
- [ ] Ground plane split drawing
- [ ] Keep-out zone definitions
- [ ] Hi-pot test point locations

## Acceptance Criteria
- Barriers provide >6mm clearance, >8mm creepage
- Slots properly routed (no copper bridges)
- No traces cross barriers except through isolators
- Hi-pot test points accessible

title: REQ-SAFE-03: Safety Marking Requirements
description:
Define safety marking requirements for PCB silkscreen.

## Required Safety Markings (IEC 60335)

### 1. High Voltage Warning
**Symbol:** Lightning bolt in triangle (IEC 60417-5036)
**Text:** "DANGER: HIGH VOLTAGE" or "CAUTION: 400V DC"
**Location:** Near DC bus capacitors, visible when enclosure open

**Size Requirements:**
- Symbol: Minimum 10mm height
- Text: Minimum 2mm character height
- Clearance: 3mm from any conductor

### 2. Protective Earth Symbol
**Symbol:** IEC 60417-5019 (earth ground)
**Location:** At PE connection point
**Size:** Minimum 5mm

### 3. Isolation Barrier Marking
**Marking:** Dashed line or solid line with text "ISOLATION BARRIER"
**Location:** Along HV-LV boundary on PCB
**Purpose:** Warn technicians during service

### 4. Polarity Markings
**Symbols:** +/- near polarized components
**Location:**
- Bus capacitors (C_BUS1, C_BUS2)
- Electrolytic capacitors
- Diodes (cathode band indication)

### 5. Pin 1 Indicators
**Symbol:** Dot, triangle, or notch
**Location:** All ICs and polarized connectors
**Purpose:** Correct assembly orientation

## Component Reference Designators

### Required Markings
| Component | Designator | Additional |
|-----------|------------|------------|
| IGBTs | Q1, Q2 | "HV" warning nearby |
| Bus Caps | C_BUS1, C_BUS2 | Polarity, "400V" |
| Fuse | F1 | Rating "15A 250V" |
| ESP32 | U_MCU | Pin 1 dot |
| Gate Driver | U_GD | "Isolation" note |

### Silkscreen Font Requirements
- Minimum character height: 1mm (0.8mm acceptable for tight spaces)
- Line width: 0.15mm minimum
- Font: Sans-serif (vector) for KiCad

## Board Identification

### Required Text
```
TEMPER v1.0
Induction Cooker Controller
Date: YYYY-MM-DD
Designer: [Name]
```

### Placement
- Location: Top layer, visible corner
- Size: 2mm characters minimum

## Connector Labeling

### All Connectors Must Be Labeled
| Connector | Label | Additional Info |
|-----------|-------|-----------------|
| J_AC_IN | "AC IN" | "L N PE" pins |
| J_COIL | "COIL OUT" | "HV" warning |
| J_RTD1 | "PAN TEMP" | |
| J_RTD2 | "IGBT TEMP" | |
| J_UI | "USER I/F" | Pin function |
| J_PROG | "PROG" | "TX RX GND 3V3" |
| J_FAN | "FAN" | "+12V GND" |

## Warning Label Placement

### Top Side Warnings
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                         │
│   ⚡ DANGER: HIGH VOLTAGE                              │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│   │              HIGH VOLTAGE ZONE                   │  │
│   │   [C_BUS1]              [C_BUS2]                │  │
│   │      +                      +                   │  │
│   │   CAUTION: 400V DC      CAUTION: 400V DC       │  │
│   │                                                 │  │
│   │   ════════ ISOLATION BARRIER ════════          │  │
│   └─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                         │
│                   [ESP32 Module]                        │
│                   TEMPER v1.0                          │
│                                                         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Deliverables
- [ ] Silkscreen drawing with all markings
- [ ] Symbol library (IEC 60417 symbols)
- [ ] Connector pinout labels
- [ ] Warning text specifications

## Acceptance Criteria
- All required safety markings present
- Markings meet minimum size requirements
- High voltage areas clearly identified
- Connector labels match schematic
- No markings over pads or vias

title: Manufacturing & DFM Requirements
description:
Sub-epic covering Design for Manufacturability (DFM) requirements.

## Scope

title: REQ-DFM-01: Component Placement for Pick-and-Place
description:
Define component placement rules for automated pick-and-place assembly.

## SMT Component Placement Rules

### General Orientation
- **ICs:** Pin 1 towards top-left or consistent orientation
- **Passives:** Consistent 0° or 90° orientation per type
- **Polarized:** Consistent polarity direction where possible

### Component Spacing

#### Minimum Spacing (SMD to SMD)
| Package | Min Pad-to-Pad | Recommended |
|---------|----------------|-------------|
| 0402 | 0.15mm | 0.25mm |
| 0603 | 0.20mm | 0.30mm |
| 0805 | 0.25mm | 0.35mm |
| SOIC | 0.25mm | 0.40mm |
| QFN | 0.25mm | 0.40mm |
| Module (ESP32) | 1.0mm | 2.0mm |

#### SMD to Through-Hole Spacing
- Minimum: 1.0mm from SMD pad edge to THT pad edge
- Recommended: 2.0mm for rework access

### Component Orientation by Type

#### Resistors/Capacitors (0603/0805)
- Orientation: 0° (horizontal) or 90° (vertical)
- Consistent within each board area
- Text readable left-to-right or bottom-to-top

#### ICs (SOIC, TQFN, SOT-23)
- Pin 1 indicator visible in silkscreen
- Consistent orientation in groups
- Sufficient clearance for rework

#### ESP32-S3-WROOM Module
- Antenna facing board edge (no copper in keep-out)
- Minimum 15mm to board edge for antenna
- Ground pour under module (except antenna area)

### Pick-and-Place Machine Constraints

#### Feeder Compatibility
| Package | Tape Width | Min Qty/Reel |
|---------|------------|--------------|
| 0402 | 8mm | 10000 |
| 0603 | 8mm | 5000 |
| 0805 | 8mm | 3000 |
| SOIC-8 | 12mm | 2500 |
| SOIC-16 | 16mm | 1000 |
| TQFN-20 | 12mm | 3000 |
| ESP32 module | Tray | 50/tray |

#### Component Height
- Maximum after reflow: 25mm (machine clearance)
- Tallest SMD: ESP32-S3-WROOM (~3.5mm)
- Verify all components fit machine specs

### Fiducial Requirements

#### Global Fiducials
- **Quantity:** 3 minimum (asymmetric placement)
- **Size:** 1.0mm copper circle
- **Mask Opening:** 2.0mm diameter
- **Clearance:** 5mm from board edge
- **No silk/copper:** 3mm radius keep-out

#### Local Fiducials
- **Where:** Near fine-pitch components (ESP32, TQFN)
- **Size:** 0.5mm copper circle
- **Placement:** Within 50mm of target component

### Panel Design (if panelized)
- Tooling holes: 3.2mm at standard locations
- V-score or tab-routed
- Panel fiducials in addition to board fiducials
- Minimum 5mm between boards

## Solder Paste Considerations

### Stencil Design
| Component | Aperture Ratio | Notes |
|-----------|----------------|-------|
| 0402 | 0.66 | Reduce by 10% |
| 0603 | 0.80 | Standard |
| 0805 | 1.00 | Standard |
| TQFN (0.5mm pitch) | 0.66 | Home plate design |
| QFN exposed pad | 0.50 | Windowpane pattern |

### Stencil Thickness
- **Recommended:** 0.12mm (5 mil) for mixed assembly
- **Alternative:** 0.15mm (6 mil) if no 0402 parts

## Deliverables
- [ ] Component placement map with orientations
- [ ] Spacing verification report
- [ ] Fiducial placement drawing
- [ ] Pick-and-place coordinate file (CSV)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All SMD spacing meets minimum requirements
- Fiducials properly placed for machine vision
- Component orientations consistent and documented
- Centroid file exports correctly from KiCad
close_reason: Created TDD tests for DFM requirements: pick_and_place.py, test_points.py validators with component spacing, orientation, fiducials, test point coverage, and accessibility checks

title: REQ-DFM-02: Test Point Accessibility
description:
Define test point locations for manufacturing test and debugging.

## Test Point Requirements

### Categories
1. **Manufacturing Test (ICT/FCT)** - Verify assembly
2. **Debug Access** - Development and troubleshooting
3. **Calibration Points** - Production calibration

## Manufacturing Test Points

### Power Rails
| Test Point | Signal | Purpose | Access |
|------------|--------|---------|--------|
| TP_5V | +5V rail | Verify buck output | Top, 1.5mm pad |
| TP_3V3 | +3.3V rail | Verify LDO output | Top, 1.5mm pad |
| TP_VBOOT | Bootstrap voltage | Gate driver power | Top, 1.0mm pad |
| TP_DC_BUS | DC bus (HV!) | Bus voltage sense | Top, 2.0mm pad |

### Ground References
| Test Point | Signal | Purpose | Access |
|------------|--------|---------|--------|
| TP_PGND | Power ground | HV ground reference | Top, 2.0mm pad |
| TP_CGND | Control ground | LV ground reference | Top, 1.5mm pad |

### Critical Signals
| Test Point | Signal | Purpose | Access |
|------------|--------|---------|--------|
| TP_SW | Switch node (HV!) | Half-bridge output | Top, 1.5mm pad |
| TP_GATE_H | High-side gate | Gate drive verify | Top, 1.0mm pad |
| TP_GATE_L | Low-side gate | Gate drive verify | Top, 1.0mm pad |
| TP_CT_OUT | CT output | Current sense | Top, 1.0mm pad |
| TP_EN | Enable signal | System enable | Top, 1.0mm pad |
| TP_FAULT | Fault output | Safety latch | Top, 1.0mm pad |

## Debug/Programming Access

### UART Programming Header (J_PROG)
| Pin | Signal | Purpose |
|-----|--------|---------|
| 1 | TX | ESP32 TX → PC RX |
| 2 | RX | ESP32 RX ← PC TX |
| 3 | EN | Reset (active low) |
| 4 | IO0 | Boot mode select |
| 5 | 3V3 | 3.3V output |
| 6 | GND | Ground |

**Header Type:** 2.54mm pitch, 6-pin
**Location:** Board edge for easy access

### Optional JTAG (J_JTAG)
| Pin | Signal |
|-----|--------|
| 1 | VCC (3.3V) |
| 2 | TMS |
| 3 | GND |
| 4 | TCK |
| 5 | GND |
| 6 | TDO |
| 7 | - |
| 8 | TDI |
| 9 | GND |
| 10 | RESET |

**Header Type:** ARM 10-pin (1.27mm pitch)
**Note:** Optional, DNP for production

## Test Point Physical Design

### Pad Sizes
| Type | Pad Size | Via (if needed) |
|------|----------|-----------------|
| HV test points | 2.0mm round | 0.5mm drill |
| LV power | 1.5mm round | 0.4mm drill |
| Signal | 1.0mm round | 0.3mm drill |
| ICT nail bed | 1.0mm round | None preferred |

### Placement Rules
1. **Minimum 2.54mm pitch** for ICT fixture pins
2. **Top side preferred** (bottom accessible for ICT)
3. **5mm from board edge** minimum
4. **Clear of tall components** (probe access)
5. **No silk over test pads**

### Test Point Grid (for ICT)
- If using ICT fixture, align to 2.54mm (100 mil) grid
- Alternatively, 1.27mm (50 mil) grid for dense boards
- Document test point coordinates in separate file

## Bed-of-Nails Considerations

### If ICT is Required
- All test points on 100 mil grid
- Top and bottom access may be needed
- 50+ test points typical for this complexity
- Coordinate file required for fixture design

### If Flying Probe Only
- Grid alignment not critical
- Fewer access points needed
- Focus on key nets only

## Deliverables
- [ ] Test point specification table
- [ ] Test point location drawing
- [ ] Coordinate file for test fixtures
- [ ] Programming header pinout

## Acceptance Criteria
- All power rails have accessible test points
- Critical signals measurable without probing ICs
- Test points don't interfere with component placement
- Programming header accessible from board edge
- HV test points clearly marked as dangerous
close_reason: Created TDD tests for DFM requirements: pick_and_place.py, test_points.py validators with component spacing, orientation, fiducials, test point coverage, and accessibility checks

title: REQ-DFM-03: Assembly Documentation Package
description:
Create complete assembly documentation for manufacturing.

## Documentation Package Contents

### 1. Assembly Drawing (PDF)
**Purpose:** Visual reference for assembly technicians

**Content:**
- Board outline with dimensions
- Component placement (top and bottom)
- Reference designators visible
- Mounting hole locations
- Connector orientations
- Polarity markings highlighted
- DNP (Do Not Place) components identified

**Format:** PDF, A3 or A4 size, scaled to fit

### 2. Bill of Materials (BOM)
**Format:** CSV or Excel

**Required Columns:**
| Column | Description | Example |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| Item | Line number | 1 |
| Qty | Quantity per board | 2 |
| Reference | Component designators | R1, R2 |
| Value | Component value | 10kΩ |
| Package | Footprint | 0603 |
| Description | Full description | Resistor, 10k, 1%, 0603 |
| Manufacturer | Preferred mfg | Yageo |
| MPN | Manufacturer part number | RC0603FR-0710KL |
| Supplier | Distributor | Digi-Key |
| Supplier PN | Distributor part number | 311-10.0KHRCT-ND |
| DNP | Do Not Place flag | No |
| Notes | Special instructions | 1% tolerance required |

### 3. Pick-and-Place File (CPL)
**Format:** CSV

**Required Columns:**
| Column | Description | Example |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| Designator | Reference | R1 |
| Mid X | X coordinate (mm) | 25.400 |
| Mid Y | Y coordinate (mm) | 38.100 |
| Layer | Top/Bottom | Top |
| Rotation | Degrees | 90 |

**Notes:**
- Coordinates relative to board origin (bottom-left)
- Rotation per manufacturer convention
- Verify coordinate system with fab house

### 4. Gerber Files (Fabrication)
**Layer Set:**
| File | Extension | Content |
|------|-----------|---------|
| Top Copper | .GTL | L1 artwork |
| Ground Plane | .G2L | L2 artwork |
| Power Plane | .G3L | L3 artwork |
| Bottom Copper | .GBL | L4 artwork |
| Top Solder Mask | .GTS | Top mask |
| Bottom Solder Mask | .GBS | Bottom mask |
| Top Silkscreen | .GTO | Top legend |
| Bottom Silkscreen | .GBO | Bottom legend |
| Top Paste | .GTP | Stencil apertures |
| Bottom Paste | .GBP | Stencil apertures |
| Board Outline | .GKO | Mechanical |
| Drill | .DRL | Excellon format |

### 5. Drill File
**Format:** Excellon (NC drill)

**Content:**
- All through-hole locations
- Via locations (if plated separate)
- Tool table with drill sizes

### 6. IPC-D-356 Netlist (Optional)
**Purpose:** Electrical test verification
**Format:** IPC-D-356A
**Content:** Net connectivity for ICT

### 7. 3D Model (STEP)
**Purpose:** Mechanical interference check
**Format:** STEP AP214

### 8. Assembly Instructions (THT)
**Purpose:** Guide for hand-soldered components

**Components for Hand Assembly:**
- Q1, Q2 (IGBTs) - TO-247
- C_BUS1, C_BUS2 - Radial capacitors
- D1, D2 - TO-220 diodes
- K_BYPASS - Relay
- L_BUCK - Through-hole inductor
- All connectors

**Instructions:**
1. Verify PCB passed SMT inspection
2. Install components in height order (shortest first)
3. Apply flux to leads
4. Solder with appropriate iron temperature (350-380°C)
5. Inspect joints for proper wetting
6. Clean flux residue if required

## Quality Checkpoints

### SMT Assembly Checklist
- [ ] Solder paste print inspection (SPI)
- [ ] Component placement verification
- [ ] Reflow profile recorded
- [ ] AOI (Automated Optical Inspection)
- [ ] Touch-up any defects

### THT Assembly Checklist
- [ ] Component identification verified
- [ ] Polarity correct (caps, diodes)
- [ ] IGBT mounting torque verified
- [ ] Solder joint visual inspection
- [ ] Heatsink assembly (if applicable)

### Final Assembly Checklist
- [ ] All connectors seated properly
- [ ] No solder bridges (magnified inspection)
- [ ] No lifted pads or traces
- [ ] Board clean (no flux residue)
- [ ] Ready for functional test

## Deliverables
- [ ] Assembly drawing (PDF)
- [ ] BOM (CSV/Excel)
- [ ] Pick-and-place file (CSV)
- [ ] Gerber package (ZIP)
- [ ] Drill file (Excellon)
- [ ] STEP model
- [ ] THT assembly instructions
- [ ] Quality checklists

## Acceptance Criteria
- All documentation complete and consistent
- BOM matches schematic component count
- Pick-and-place coordinates verified
- Gerber files pass DFM check at fab house
- 3D model shows no interferences
close_reason: Created TDD tests: validators/documentation.py with BOM completeness, CPL coordinates, Gerber layers, and DNP consistency validation

title: Design Review Process
description:
Sub-epic covering design review checklists and sign-off criteria.

## Scope
- Schematic review checklist
- Layout review checklist
- DRC/ERC verification process
- Pre-fabrication sign-off criteria
- Design freeze process

## Review Stages
1. Schematic Review (before layout)
2. Placement Review (component placement complete)
3. Routing Review (routing complete)
4. Final Review (before fab release)

## Participants
- Design engineer (primary)
- Peer review (secondary)
- AI agent verification (automated checks)

## Reference Documents
- IPC-2221B design standard
- IPC-7351B land pattern standard
- KiCad DRC/ERC rules

## Success Criteria
- All checklists completed with sign-off
- DRC zero errors, ERC zero errors
- All review comments resolved
- Fab files verified with Gerber viewer
close_reason: Completed all design review and sign-off criteria tasks.

title: REQ-REV-01: Schematic Review Checklist
description:
Schematic review checklist to be completed before layout begins.

## Pre-Layout Schematic Review

### 1. Power Supply Verification
- [ ] All ICs have correct supply voltage (3.3V vs 5V)
- [ ] Decoupling capacitors on every power pin
- [ ] Bulk capacitors at power entry points
- [ ] Power sequencing correct (if required)
- [ ] Current ratings adequate for all supplies
- [ ] Voltage ratings adequate for all components

### 2. Component Selection
- [ ] All components have valid part numbers
- [ ] Footprints assigned and verified
- [ ] Temperature ratings adequate (125°C for power, 85°C for logic)
- [ ] Voltage ratings include safety margin (>20%)
- [ ] Current ratings include safety margin (>30%)
- [ ] No obsolete or EOL parts

### 3. Net Naming and Hierarchy
- [ ] All nets have meaningful names
- [ ] Power nets consistently named (e.g., +5V, +3V3, GND)
- [ ] No duplicate net names with different meanings
- [ ] Hierarchical sheet connections correct
- [ ] Global labels used appropriately

### 4. Safety Circuit Review
- [ ] OCP circuit values verified
- [ ] OVP circuit values verified
- [ ] Thermal shutdown thresholds correct
- [ ] Gate driver enable/disable logic correct
- [ ] Watchdog timer configured
- [ ] Fault latch operation verified

### 5. Analog Circuit Review
- [ ] ADC input ranges correct (0-3.3V for ESP32)
- [ ] CT burden resistor value verified
- [ ] NTC thermistor divider values correct
- [ ] Filter cutoff frequencies appropriate
- [ ] Op-amp supply rails adequate

### 6. Digital Interface Review
- [ ] SPI signals correct (CLK, MOSI, MISO, CS polarity)
- [ ] I2C pull-up resistors present
- [ ] UART TX/RX correctly assigned
- [ ] GPIO assignments documented
- [ ] PWM outputs to correct pins

### 7. Gate Drive Circuit Review
- [ ] UCC21550 input logic matches ESP32 levels
- [ ] Dead-time requirements documented
- [ ] Bootstrap capacitor value adequate
- [ ] Gate resistor values verified
- [ ] Miller clamp threshold correct

### 8. Power Stage Review
- [ ] IGBT voltage rating adequate (1200V > 340V × 2.5)
- [ ] IGBT current rating adequate (40A > 22A × 1.5)
- [ ] Snubber component values verified
- [ ] Bus capacitor ripple current rating adequate
- [ ] Resonant tank component values per simulation

### 9. Connectivity Verification
- [ ] ERC passes with zero errors
- [ ] No unconnected pins (or explicitly marked NC)
- [ ] No floating inputs
- [ ] All test points connected
- [ ] All connectors have correct pinouts

### 10. Documentation
- [ ] All component values on schematic
- [ ] Critical notes added (e.g., DNP, matched pair)
- [ ] Net classes assigned
- [ ] Power dissipation noted for power components
- [ ] Reference documents linked

## Sign-Off

| Reviewer | Date | Status | Comments |
|----------|------|--------|----------|
| Design Engineer | | [ ] Pass [ ] Fail | |
| Peer Review | | [ ] Pass [ ] Fail | |

## Deliverables
- [ ] Completed checklist with sign-off
- [ ] ERC report (zero errors)
- [ ] Updated schematic if issues found
- [ ] Review meeting notes

## Acceptance Criteria
- All checklist items verified
- ERC passes with zero errors
- All reviewers sign off
- Issues resolved before layout starts
close_reason: Created comprehensive TDD test suite for schematic review checklist. Implemented 27 tests covering all requirements: power supply verification (8 tests), component selection (7 tests), net naming (5 tests), safety circuits (5 tests), and integration tests (2 tests). Validator module with 21 functions created in tests/requirements/validators/schematic.py. All tests pass with NotImplementedError (TDD approach). See tests/requirements/review/test_schematic.py and README.md for details.

title: REQ-REV-02: Layout Review Checklist
description:
Layout review checklist to be completed before fabrication release.

## Layout Review Checklist

### 1. Board Outline and Mechanical
- [ ] Board dimensions correct (100mm × 150mm)
- [ ] Corner radii correct (3mm)
- [ ] Mounting holes in correct locations (5mm from edge)
- [ ] Keep-outs around mounting holes (3mm radius)
- [ ] Board thickness specified (1.6mm)
- [ ] Height constraints respected per zone

### 2. Layer Stack Verification
- [ ] 4-layer stackup correct (Top/GND/PWR/Bot)
- [ ] Copper weights correct (2oz outer, 1oz inner)
- [ ] Prepreg/core thicknesses specified
- [ ] Controlled impedance not required (verified)

### 3. Component Placement
- [ ] All components placed
- [ ] No component overlaps (3D check)
- [ ] Clearance for heatsink mounting
- [ ] IGBTs on board edge for heatsink
- [ ] ESP32 antenna area clear (15mm keep-out)
- [ ] Connectors accessible from board edge
- [ ] Test points accessible

### 4. Critical Loops (per REQ-ELEC-06)
- [ ] DC bus loop area <5 cm²
- [ ] Gate drive loops <2 cm²
- [ ] Buck converter SW loop <1 cm²
- [ ] Bootstrap loop minimized
- [ ] CT burden loop minimized

### 5. Ground Plane Integrity (per REQ-EMC-01)
- [ ] No slots >30mm in ground plane
- [ ] Signal traces over solid ground
- [ ] Star ground point implemented
- [ ] Via stitching at 5mm along boundaries
- [ ] PGND/CGND/ISOGND properly separated

### 6. Power Distribution
- [ ] Power plane islands correctly defined
- [ ] No power island under SW nodes
- [ ] Via stitching on power islands
- [ ] Current capacity adequate for traces

### 7. Clearance and Creepage (per REQ-SAFE-01)
- [ ] HV to LV clearance >6mm
- [ ] HV to LV creepage >8mm
- [ ] Isolation slots routed
- [ ] UCC21550 barrier respected
- [ ] ADUM1250 barrier respected

### 8. Thermal Management (per REQ-THERM)
- [ ] Thermal via arrays under IGBTs
- [ ] Thermal via arrays under LMR51430
- [ ] Copper pours for heat spreading
- [ ] Thermal relief settings correct
- [ ] No thermal isolated pads

### 9. EMC Layout (per REQ-EMC)
- [ ] Bypass caps within 3mm of IC pins
- [ ] Ground vias at cap pads
- [ ] EMI filter component placement correct
- [ ] SW node area minimized
- [ ] No power plane under SW nodes

### 10. Silkscreen and Markings
- [ ] All reference designators visible
- [ ] Safety warnings present
- [ ] Polarity markings correct
- [ ] Pin 1 indicators present
- [ ] Board revision marked
- [ ] No silkscreen over pads

### 11. Manufacturing Readiness
- [ ] Fiducials present (3 global, local near ESP32)
- [ ] Minimum trace/space rules met (6/6 mil)
- [ ] Minimum via specs met (0.3mm drill)
- [ ] Component spacing adequate for pick-and-place
- [ ] Solder paste layer correct

### 12. DRC/ERC Final Check
- [ ] DRC passes with zero errors
- [ ] All unconnected nets intentional
- [ ] All net classes assigned
- [ ] All design rules applied

## Sign-Off

| Reviewer | Date | Status | Comments |
|----------|------|--------|----------|
| Design Engineer | | [ ] Pass [ ] Fail | |
| Peer Review | | [ ] Pass [ ] Fail | |
| DFM Check (Fab) | | [ ] Pass [ ] Fail | |

## Deliverables
- [ ] Completed checklist with sign-off
- [ ] DRC report (zero errors)
- [ ] 3D model screenshot (interference check)
- [ ] Review meeting notes

## Acceptance Criteria
- All checklist items verified
- DRC passes with zero errors
- All reviewers sign off
- Ready for Gerber generation
close_reason: Created comprehensive layout review checklist in docs/LAYOUT_REVIEW_CHECKLIST.md.

title: REQ-REV-03: Pre-Fabrication Sign-Off
description:
Final sign-off checklist before releasing design to fabrication.

## Pre-Fabrication Verification

### 1. Design File Verification
- [ ] Final schematic exported (PDF)
- [ ] Final layout exported (PDF)
- [ ] Gerber files generated
- [ ] Drill files generated
- [ ] Pick-and-place file generated
- [ ] BOM finalized

### 2. Gerber File Inspection
- [ ] Opened in Gerber viewer (GerbV, KiCad viewer, etc.)
- [ ] All layers present and aligned
- [ ] Board outline correct
- [ ] Drill file matches pad locations
- [ ] Solder mask openings correct
- [ ] Silkscreen readable and complete
- [ ] No stray geometry or artifacts

### 3. BOM Verification
- [ ] All components have part numbers
- [ ] All components in stock (or lead time acceptable)
- [ ] No DNP discrepancies
- [ ] Quantities correct
- [ ] Alternates documented if needed

### 4. Design Rule Summary
Confirm these are set correctly in design and communicated to fab:

| Rule | Value | Verified |
|------|-------|----------|
| Min trace width | 0.15mm (6 mil) | [ ] |
| Min trace space | 0.15mm (6 mil) | [ ] |
| Min via drill | 0.3mm (12 mil) | [ ] |
| Min annular ring | 0.15mm (6 mil) | [ ] |
| Outer copper | 2 oz (70 µm) | [ ] |
| Inner copper | 1 oz (35 µm) | [ ] |
| Board thickness | 1.6mm | [ ] |
| Surface finish | ENIG | [ ] |
| Solder mask | Green or Black | [ ] |
| Silkscreen | White | [ ] |

### 5. Special Manufacturing Notes
- [ ] Controlled impedance: Not required
- [ ] Via fill: Required for thermal pads (specify in notes)
- [ ] Edge plating: Not required
- [ ] Slots: Specify in drill file or separate file
- [ ] Panel: Individual board (no panel)

### 6. Fab House Pre-Check (if available)
- [ ] Upload to fab house DFM checker
- [ ] Review any warnings
- [ ] Resolve or accept warnings with justification
- [ ] Quote matches expectations

### 7. Revision Control
- [ ] Design version correct on board
- [ ] Design version in filename
- [ ] Git commit tagged with release
- [ ] Change log updated

### 8. Final Checklist Completion
- [ ] Schematic review checklist complete (REQ-REV-01)
- [ ] Layout review checklist complete (REQ-REV-02)
- [ ] All blocking issues resolved
- [ ] All warnings documented and accepted

## Fabrication Order Information

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Board Name | TEMPER v1.0 |
| Quantity | [To be determined] |
| Layer Count | 4 |
| Dimensions | 100mm × 150mm |
| Material | FR4 TG150 |
| Copper | 2oz/1oz/1oz/1oz |
| Finish | ENIG |
| Solder Mask | [Green/Black] |
| Silkscreen | White |
| Lead Time | [Standard/Rush] |

## Sign-Off

**I confirm that this design has been reviewed and is ready for fabrication:**

| Role | Name | Date | Signature |
|------|------|------|-----------|
| Design Engineer | | | |
| Project Lead | | | |

## Deliverables
- [ ] Gerber package (ZIP file)
- [ ] Drill file
- [ ] BOM (final version)
- [ ] Pick-and-place file
- [ ] Assembly drawing
- [ ] README with fabrication notes

## Acceptance Criteria
- All verification steps completed
- All sign-offs obtained
- Files uploaded to fab house
- Order confirmation received
